category_title: Six-year-old students with EBD, ADHD, and ODD who exhibit noncompliance, aggression, and disrobing during difficult academic tasks
minimal_context: Axel is sitting in his individual work area being asked to complete a multistep academic worksheet.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Axel, it is time for your worksheet. First, write your name at the top, and then circle the pictures.

STUDENT: No! I'm not doing it!

TUTOR: You need to follow the directions and do your work.

STUDENT: (Throws his pencil out of the work area) It's too hard!

TUTOR: Please stop throwing things and start your assignment.

STUDENT: (Screams a swear word, removes his shoes and socks, and makes an obscene gesture at the tutor)

options.A: Place the consequence maps on a table or the ground in front of Axel, point to the consequence maps, provide positive praise statements for demonstrating appropriate calm down behaviors, and then prompt Axel to point to the corresponding boxes on the calm down schedule.
options.B: Maintain close physical proximity to Axel (within 0.3 meters) in the individual work area during all interactions regarding the multistep academic worksheet for the duration of the session.
options.C: Position physically away from Axel in the individual work area, providing support with the multistep academic worksheet only intermittently rather than constantly throughout the remainder of the session.
options.D: Use a request for clarification in the individual work area, asking Axel for explanations and elaborations regarding the multistep academic worksheet while he is in the middle of the session.
